texto: You can never replace someone you love like that. Eventually the memories won’t be so hard on you. With time, the memories will make you smile and you will be grateful for having them.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a N.M. Facile: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “You can never replace someone you love like that. Eventually the memories won’t be so hard on you…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que gratidao, love, memory pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de memory.

frase_original: You can never replace someone you love like that. Eventually the memories won’t be so hard on you. With time, the memories will make you smile and you will be grateful for having them.

texto: This isn’t over, Sylvia. I’ll leave you now, but I’m watching. I will not let that bastard hurt you.” I said this just as I reached the door. As she shut the door I heard a strangled whisper, “No, you’re perfectly capable of doing that on your own.

frase_original: This isn’t over, Sylvia. I’ll leave you now, but I’m watching. I will not let that bastard hurt you.” I said this just as I reached the door. As she shut the door I heard a strangled whisper, “No, you’re perfectly capable of doing that on your own.
